blocks.content: I asked her something real. She told me something real back. That's all it took and I'd been sitting on the steps for three weeks before I did it.
I almost said it. The words were right there and I talked around them and she got on the bus and I watched it go and thought about all the things I could have said.
I was going to do it when she stood up. She stood up. I didn't. I still don't know exactly why.
I didn't ask. I don't know why I didn't ask. I know why. I just can't say it right.
Down the hall Larry Jr.'s light was off. Mr. Cole's too.
The house was enormous and quiet and mine.

options.value: You ran like you had something to prove.
You got in your own way.
The moment got big on you.
You asked. She had the pen ready.
You told him some of it.
You went quiet and he waited.
You told her. She already knew.
Two seconds of something neither of you named.

scenes.text: First game. The field looks different under the lights.
Daniel Snyder. A different hallway. A different kind of quiet.
"You can't just keep showing up to the steps, Larry."
